```tsx
              type="merke"
              title="Clinical pearl"
              content={
                <>
                  Most patients with{" "}
                  <ContentLink href="www.google.com" target="_blank">
                    Turner syndrome
                  </ContentLink>{" "}
                  have normal intelligence.<sup>[19]</sup>
                </>
              }
            />
            <ContentBox
              type="merkspruch"
              title="Student pearl"
              content={
                <>
                  The{" "}
                  <ContentText weight="bold" as="span">
                    L
                  </ContentText>
                  ingula in is the{" "}
                  <ContentText weight="bold" as="span">
                    L
                  </ContentText>
                  eft{" "}
                  <ContentText weight="bold" as="span">
                    L
                  </ContentText>
                  ung
                </>
              }
            />
          </Stack>
        </Column>
        <Column size={6}>
          <Stack>
            <ContentHeading level="H2">DE Market</ContentHeading>
            <ContentBox
              type="cave"
              title="Cave"
              content={
                <>
                  Die Leitlinienempfehlungen hinsichtlich der
                  Untersuchingsintervalle unterscheiden sich teilweise von den
                  vergegebenen Intervallen im Rahmen des{" "}
                  <ContentLink href="www.google.com" target="_blank">
                    DMP Diabetes mellitus!
                  </ContentLink>
                </>
              }
            />
            <ContentBox
              type="dgim-negative"
              title="DGIM Negative Empfehlung"
              content={
                <>
                  In der Frühphase einer akuten{" "}
                  <ContentLink href="www.google.com" target="_blank">
                    Pankreatitis
                  </ContentLink>{" "}
                  sollte eine{" "}
                  <ContentLink href="www.google.com" target="_blank">
                    Computertomografie
                  </ContentLink>{" "}
                  night angefertift werden! (
                  <ContentLink href="www.google.com" target="_blank">
                    DGIM - Klug entscheiden in der Notaufnahme
                  </ContentLink>
                  )
                </>
              }
            />
            <ContentBox
              type="dgim-positive"
              title="DGIM Positive Empfehlung"
              content={
                <>
                  In der Frühphase einer akuten{" "}
                  <ContentLink href="www.google.com" target="_blank">
                    Pankreatitis
                  </ContentLink>{" "}
                  sollte eine{" "}
                  <ContentLink href="www.google.com" target="_blank">
                    Computertomografie
                  </ContentLink>{" "}
                  night angefertift werden! (
                  <ContentLink href="www.google.com" target="_blank">
                    DGIM - Klug entscheiden in der Notaufnahme
                  </ContentLink>
                  )
                </>
              }
            />
            <ContentBox
              type="merke"
              title="Merke"
              content={
                <>
                  Zur Einschätzung des Vorleigens einer möglichen{" "}
                  <ContentLink href="www.google.com" target="_blank">
                    Beckenfraktur
                  </ContentLink>{" "}
                  kann dass KISS-Schema helfen:{" "}
                  <ContentText weight="bold" as="span">
                    K
                  </ContentText>
                  inematik des Traumas,{" "}
                  <ContentText weight="bold" as="span">
                    I
                  </ContentText>
                  nspektionsbefund,{" "}
                  <ContentText weight="bold" as="span">
                    S
                  </ContentText>
                  chmerzen,{" "}
                  <ContentText weight="bold" as="span">
                    S
                  </ContentText>
                  tabilisierung!
                </>
              }
            />
            <ContentBox
              type="case-link"
              title="Fall"
              content={
                <ContentLink href="www.google.com" target="_blank">
                  Älterer Mann mit Dyspnoe
                </ContentLink>
              }
            />
            <ContentBox
              type="merkspruch"
              title="Merkspruch"
              content={
                <>
                  <ContentText weight="bold" as="span">
                    A
                  </ContentText>
                  nton{" "}
                  <ContentText weight="bold" as="span">
                    Pul
                  </ContentText>
                  mann{" "}
                  <ContentText weight="bold" as="span">
                    tri
                  </ContentText>
                  nkt{" "}
                  <ContentText weight="bold" as="span">
                    Mi
                  </ContentText>
                  lch um 22:45 Uhr.
                </>
              }
            />
            <ContentBox
              type="clinic-box"
              title="Klinik Kasten"
              content={
                <>
                  <ContentText weight="bold" as="span">
                    Pancoast-Tumor
                  </ContentText>

                  <ContentLink href="www.google.com" target="_blank">
                    Maligne Tumoren
                  </ContentLink>{" "}
                  der Lungenspitze (
                  <ContentLink href="www.google.com" target="_blank">
                    Pancoast-Tumoren
                  </ContentLink>
                  ) können wegen ihrer anatomischen Lagebeziehungen zu
                  speziellen Symptomen führen.
                </>
              }
            />
            <ContentBox
              type="guideline-recommendations"
              title="Empfehlung"
              content={
                <>
                  <b>DGEM-Intensivmedizin-Empfehlung 2:</b> Der
                  Ernährungszustand sollte zum Zeitpunkt der Aufnahme auf die
                  Intensivstation abgeschätzt werden. Starker Konsens (97%)
                </>
              }
            />
          </Stack>
        </Column>
      </Columns>
    </div>
```

## WithContentList

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 944 }}>
      <ContentBox
        type="merke"
        title="Clinical pearl"
        content={
          <>
            <ContentText>Key diagnostic criteria:</ContentText>
            <ContentList>
              <li>
                <ContentText>
                  Elevated{" "}
                  <ContentLink href="www.google.com" target="_blank">
                    blood pressure
                  </ContentLink>{" "}
                  on at least two separate occasions
                </ContentText>
              </li>
              <li>
                <ContentText>Proteinuria &gt; 300 mg/24h</ContentText>
              </li>
              <li>
                <ContentText>
                  End-organ damage (e.g., retinopathy, nephropathy)
                </ContentText>
              </li>
            </ContentList>
          </>
        }
      />
    </div>
```

**Type Definitions:**

```typescript
export type ContentBoxProps = {
  type: ContentBoxType;
  content: string | ReactNode;
  title: string;
  "data-e2e-test-id"?: string;
  renderTopRightElement?: () => React.ReactNode;
};

type StyledContainerProps = {
  colorVariant: ContentBoxColorVariant;
};
```


### ContentFontResizer

# ContentFontResizer

A context provider component that provides a `fontSizeOffset` value to its children.

## BasicContentFontResizer

```tsx
<ContentFontResizer {...args}>
      <ContentHeading level="H1">Heading 1</ContentHeading>
      <ContentText>
        Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet consectetur adipisicing elit. Maiores nulla
        deleniti illum libero harum consectetur, doloribus voluptates voluptatem
        reiciendis porro mollitia amet corrupti. Aspernatur, fuga repellendus.
        Officia rem ex earum.
      </ContentText>
    </ContentFontResizer>
```

## With Markdown content

This is an example of how streaming content in markdown can be rendered with Content components. This markdown is from AI mode output.

```tsx
{
    const options = {
      overrides: {
        a: {
          component: ({
            href,
            // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars
            color,
            ...rest
          }: ComponentPropsWithoutRef<"a">) => {
            if (href && href.includes("type=lisa_reference")) {
              const referenceNum = href.match(/referenceNum=(\d+)/)?.[1] ?? "";

              return <ReferenceButton>{referenceNum}</ReferenceButton>;
            }
            return <ContentLink href={href} {...rest} />;
          },
        },
        p: {
          component: ContentText,
          props: {
            isSpacedOut: true,
          },
        },
        strong: {
          component: ContentText,
          props: {
            weight: "bold",
            as: "span",
            isSpacedOut: true,
          },
        },
        ul: {
          component: ContentList,
        },
        li: {
          component: ({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) => (
            <li>
              <ContentText isSpacedOut>{children}</ContentText>
            </li>
          ),
        },
        h3: {
          component: ContentHeading,
          props: {
            level: "H3",
          },
        },
        h4: {
          component: ContentHeading,
          props: {
            level: "H4",
          },
        },
        table: {
          component: ContentTable,
        },
        th: {
          component: ({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) => (
            <th>
              <ContentText weight="bold" isSpacedOut>
                {children}
              </ContentText>
            </th>
          ),
        },
        td: {
          component: ({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) => (
            <td>
              <ContentText isSpacedOut>{children}</ContentText>
            </td>
          ),
        },
      },
    };

    return (
      <StyledContainer>
        <ContentFontResizer {...args}>
          <Markdown options={options}>{content}</Markdown>
        </ContentFontResizer>
      </StyledContainer>
    )
```

**Type Definitions:**

```typescript
export type ContentFontResizerProps = {
  children: React.ReactNode;
  /** Increment/Decrement font size by given offset */
  fontSizeOffset: number;
  "data-e2e-test-id"?: string;
};
```


### ContentHeading

# ContentHeading

## BasicContentHeading

```tsx
<ContentHeading {...args}>
      Text with <ContentLink href="#">Some link</ContentLink>{" "}
    </ContentHeading>
```

**Type Definitions:**

```typescript
export type ContentHeadingProps = TypographyHeaderProps & {
  color?: ContentColor | HeaderProps["color"];
  /** HTML heading level to render */
  level: HeadingLevel;
  hasDivider?: boolean;
  wrapperClassName?: string;
};

type StyledHeaderProps = TypographyHeaderProps & {
  fontSizeOffset: FontSizeOffset;
  color: ContentHeadingProps["color"];
};
```


### ContentLink

# ContentLink

## BasicContentLink

```tsx
<ContentText>
      <ContentLink {...args}>Test link</ContentLink>
    </ContentText>
```

**Type Definitions:**

```typescript
export type ContentLinkProps = Omit<LinkProps, "color"> & {
  color?: ContentColor | LinkProps["color"];
  isDotted?: boolean;
  /** Provides additional line-height, inherits from ContentText if not provided */
  isSpacedOut?: boolean;
};

type StyledLinkProps = LinkProps &
  Pick<ContentLinkProps, "isDotted" | "isSpacedOut"> & {
    fontSizeOffset: FontSizeOffset;
    contentColor?: ContentColor;
  };
```


### ContentList

# ContentList

## UnorderedList

```tsx
<ContentList {...args}>
      <li>
        <ContentText>First item</ContentText>
      </li>
      <li>
        <ContentText>Second item</ContentText>
      </li>
      <li>
        <ContentText>Third item</ContentText>
      </li>
    </ContentList>
```

## OrderedList

```tsx
<ContentList {...args}>
      <li>
        <ContentText>First item</ContentText>
      </li>
      <li>
        <ContentText>Second item</ContentText>
      </li>
      <li>
        <ContentText>Third item</ContentText>
      </li>
    </ContentList>
```

## CustomListStyle

```tsx
<ContentList {...args}>
      <li>
        <ContentText>Alpha item</ContentText>
      </li>
      <li>
        <ContentText>Beta item</ContentText>
      </li>
      <li>
        <ContentText>Gamma item</ContentText>
      </li>
    </ContentList>
```

## NestedList

```tsx
<ContentList {...args}>
      <li>
        <ContentText>Parent item 1</ContentText>
        <ContentList>
          <li>
            <ContentText>Nested child 1 (level 1)</ContentText>
          </li>
          <li>
            <ContentText>Nested child 2 (level 1)</ContentText>
            <ContentList>
              <li>
                <ContentText>Nested child 1 (level 2)</ContentText>
              </li>
              <li>
                <ContentText>Nested child 2 (level 2)</ContentText>
              </li>
            </ContentList>
          </li>
        </ContentList>
      </li>
      <li>
        <ContentText>Parent item 2</ContentText>
      </li>
    </ContentList>
```

## WithContentLinks

```tsx
<ContentList {...args}>
      <li>
        <ContentText>
          Item with a <ContentLink href="#">content link</ContentLink>
        </ContentText>
      </li>
      <li>
        <ContentText>
          Another item with a{" "}
          <ContentLink href="#" color="physician">
            physician link
          </ContentLink>
        </ContentText>
      </li>
    </ContentList>
```

**Type Definitions:**

```typescript
export type ContentListProps = {
  children: React.ReactNode;
  /** Render as ordered (ol) or unordered (ul) list. */
  as?: "ol" | "ul";
  /** CSS list-style-type value. */
  listStyle?: Property.ListStyleType;
  /** Display type. `checkList` renders checkboxes, `referenceList` renders numbered badges. */
  type?: ContentListVariant;
  "data-e2e-test-id"?: string;
} & Omit<React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<"ul">, "children">;

type StyledListProps = Pick<ContentListProps, "listStyle" | "type">;

export function getReferenceListBulletStyle(theme: Theme): CSSObject {
  return {
    display: "inline-flex",
    alignItems: "center",
    justifyContent: "center",
    flexShrink: 0,
    width: 16,
    height: 16,
    borderRadius: 2,
    backgroundColor:
      theme.values.color.content.referenceButton.background.default,
    fontFamily: theme.variables.fontFamily.lato,
    fontSize: 9,
    fontWeight: theme.variables.weight.normal,
    color: theme.values.color.text.primary.default,
  };
}
```


### ContentPictogramButton

# ContentPictogramButton.

Extends the PictogramButton with our additional content colors

## Default

```tsx
<ContentPictogramButton {...args} />
```

## All colors

```tsx
<div style={{ width: "250px" }}>
      <Stack fullWidth>
        <Inline alignItems="spaceBetween">
          <Text>Default</Text>
          <ContentPictogramButton {...args} />
        </Inline>
        <Inline alignItems="spaceBetween">
          <Text>Physician</Text>
          <ContentPictogramButton {...args} iconColor="physician" />
        </Inline>
        <Inline alignItems="spaceBetween">
          <Text>Physician Unimportant</Text>
          <ContentPictogramButton {...args} iconColor="physicianUnimportant" />
        </Inline>
        <Inline alignItems="spaceBetween">
          <Text>Unimportant</Text>
          <ContentPictogramButton {...args} iconColor="unimportant" />
        </Inline>
      </Stack>
    </div>
```

**Type Definitions:**

```typescript
type ContentPictogramButtonProps = Omit<
  PictogramButtonProps,
  "ariaAttributes"
> & {
  "aria-label"?: string;
  "aria-labelledby"?: string;
  iconColor?: ContentColor;
};

type StyledPictogramButtonProps = Pick<
  ContentPictogramButtonProps,
  "iconColor" | "disabled"
>;

const StyledPictogramComponent = styled(
  PictogramButton
)<StyledPictogramButtonProps>(({ theme, iconColor, disabled }) => ({
  ...(iconColor && {
    color: theme.values.color.content.icon[iconColor].default,
    ...(!disabled && {
      "&:active": {
        color: theme.values.color.content.icon[iconColor].hover,
      },
    }),
  }),
}));
```


### ContentTable

# ContentTable

## BasicTable

```tsx
<StyledContainer>
      <ContentTable {...args}>
        <thead>
          <tr>
            <th>
              <ContentText>Category</ContentText>
            </th>
            <th>
              <ContentText>Description</ContentText>
            </th>
          </tr>
        </thead>
        <tbody>
          <tr>
            <td>
              <ContentText>Microcytic</ContentText>
            </td>
            <td>
              <ContentText>MCV &lt; 80 fL</ContentText>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr>
            <td>
              <ContentText>Normocytic</ContentText>
            </td>
            <td>
              <ContentText>MCV 80–100 fL</ContentText>
            </td>
          </tr>
          <tr>
            <td>
              <ContentText>Macrocytic</ContentText>
            </td>
            <td>
              <ContentText>MCV &gt; 100 fL</ContentText>
            </td>
          </tr>
        </tbody>
      </ContentTable>
    </StyledContainer>
```

**Type Definitions:**

```typescript
export type ContentTableProps = {
  "data-e2e-test-id"?: string;
  wrapperClassName?: string;
} & React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<"table">;
```


### ContentText

# ContentText

## Basic Content text

## Spaced out Content text

Text with a larger line height used for reading content

**Type Definitions:**

```typescript
export type ContentTextProps = TypographyTextProps & {
  color?: ContentColor | TextProps["color"];
  /** Provides additional line-height */
  isSpacedOut?: boolean;
};

type StyledTextProps = TypographyTextProps & {
  fontSizeOffset: FontSizeOffset;
  contentColor?: ContentColor;
} & Pick<ContentTextProps, "isSpacedOut">;
```


### DosageButton

# DosageButton

## Default

```tsx
<ContentText isSpacedOut>
      Some content text with a dosage button <DosageButton>DOSAGE</DosageButton>{" "}
      and more content text.
    </ContentText>
```

## Resized content

```tsx
<ContentFontResizer fontSizeOffset={2}>
      <ContentText isSpacedOut>
        Some resized content text with a dosage button{" "}
        <DosageButton>DOSAGE</DosageButton> and more content text.
      </ContentText>
    </ContentFontResizer>
```

**Type Definitions:**

```typescript
type StyledContentProps = {
  fontSizeOffset: number;
};
```


### ReferenceButton

# ReferenceButton

## Default

```tsx
<ContentText>
      Some content text with a reference <ReferenceButton>1</ReferenceButton>
      <ReferenceButton>10</ReferenceButton>
    </ContentText>
```

**Type Definitions:**

```typescript
export type ReferenceButtonProps = React.ComponentPropsWithRef<"button">;

const StyledContent = styled.span(({ theme }) => ({
  ...getReferenceListBulletStyle(theme),
}));
```


## Form

### CheckboxCard

# CheckboxCard

A checkbox card is an extended input control, allowing users to check the option that works best for them.
The extended card allows us to show more information such as a status and accompanying text.

This component is still a WIP. We hope to add sizing support in the future.

## Anatomy

1. **Label**
   The primary text of the card. This is the main content that describes the option. It appears in bold and changes color when selected.
2. **Checkbox**
   The checkbox input control that allows users to select or deselect the card. Clicking anywhere on the card will toggle the checkbox state.
3. **Custom content area**
   An optional area on the right side of the card where custom content can be rendered using the `renderContent` prop. This could include icons, badges, or other UI elements.
4. **Label hint** (optional)
   Optional secondary text that provides additional context or detailed information to complement the label. Appears below the main label in a smaller font size.
5. **Card container**
   The entire card area that acts as a clickable surface. The background and border change appearance when the card is selected or hovered. It comes in two different sizes: `s` and `m` (Default)

## Props

## Basic checkbox card

A simple checkbox card with just a label.

## Size S

A smaller version of the checkbox card using the `s` size variant.

## With label hint

A checkbox card that includes additional context through the optional `labelHint` prop.

## With long label and hint

Demonstrates how the checkbox card handles longer text content for both the main label and hint text.

## With form field group

Shows multiple checkbox cards grouped together using FormFieldGroup, with custom content rendered via the `renderContent` prop to display relevant icons.

**Type Definitions:**

```typescript
export type CheckboxCardProps = {
  /* Primary text of the card. Placed next to the checkbox element. */
  label: string;
  /* Secondary text of the card. */
  labelHint?: string;
  readOnly?: boolean;
  renderContent?: (params: { isSelected: boolean }) => React.ReactNode;
} & Omit<
```


### Checkbox

# Checkbox

A checkbox is an input control that allows user to choose none, one or more options from a limited set of choices.

## Anatomy

1. **Checkbox**
   The selection control. It also displays whether an option has been selected or not.
2. **Checkbox label**
   Use this text label to describe what the checkbox is for. Keep the labels short and descriptive.
3. **Label hint (optional)**
   An additional text element can supplement the checkbox label when a more detailed description is needed.
4. **Group label**
   When presented in a group, a group label can be used to explain the group’s context.
5. **Group hint**
   Additional information accompanying a checkbox group.
6. **Group error message**
   An error message for a checkbox group. The message should clearly describe what is wrong.

## Props

## General properties & Examples

- Checkboxes are available in two different sizes, M (default) and S.
- The interactive area of a checkbox includes its label and label hint.
- Long checkbox labels will be wrapped into multiple lines.
- Label hints can contain links and will wrap into multiple lines if long.
- A checkbox has 4 different states: Unchecked, Checked, Indeterminate (for nested checkbox group) and Disabled.

### Basic checkbox

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <Checkbox {...props} />
    </div>
```

### Size S

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <Checkbox {...props} />
    </div>
```

### With label hint

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <Checkbox {...props} />
    </div>
```

### Checkbox group

Use <a href="/?path=/docs/form-formfieldgroup--docs" target="_blank">FormFieldGroup</a> to wrap a list of checkboxes. FormFieldGroup accepts an array of error messages that will be displayed in stack.

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <FormFieldGroup
        label="Group label"
        hint="Optional group hint is visible even if error is present."
        disabled={args.disabled}
        errorMessages={["Group error message 1.", "Group error message 2."]}
      >
        <Checkbox label="Option 1" labelHint="Optional label hint" name="one" />
        <Checkbox label="Option 2" labelHint="Optional label hint" name="two" />
        <Checkbox label="Option 3" name="three" />
      </FormFieldGroup>
    </div>
```

### Nested checkbox group

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <FormFieldGroup label="Checkbox group">
        <IndeterminateCheckboxes />
      </FormFieldGroup>
    </div>
```

<SelectionControls />

**Type Definitions:**

```typescript
export type CheckboxProps = {
  label?: string | React.ReactElement;
  indeterminate?: boolean;
  size?: CheckboxSizes; // TODO: This needs to be renamed as it overrides size from HTML attributes
} & Omit<FormFieldProps, "hideLabel" | "label"> &

type CheckboxRawProps = CheckboxProps & { labelSize?: CheckboxSizes };
```


### Combobox

# Combobox

A combobox combines an input field with a select option list, allowing users to filter and choose one or multiple options from a list of choices. It is particularly useful when the list of options contains a large number of items.

## Anatomy

1. **Field label**
   Describes the purpose of the select input.
2. **Text input field**
   When clicked, the list of choices appears. Once an option is selected, it is shown in the text input field. Users can type a keyword to filter the list.
3. **Label hint (optional)**
   Additional text that supplements the field label when more context is needed.
4. **Option list**
   Contains all selectable items. Optional descriptions can be displayed for each item.
5. **Selected option indicator**
   A checkmark is shown next to the selected option in a single-selection combobox. In a multi-selection variant, checkboxes in front of the options act as selection indicators.
6. **Hint**
   Can be used to provide additional information to help users make their choice faster.
7. **Error message**
   Clearly describes what is wrong when there is an error.

## Props

## General properties & Examples

- The width of a combobox is defined by its parent container.
- The label and placeholder text can be defined.
- When users type in the in, the option list is filtered to show items that match the typed characters.
- By default, the option list container has a max-height of 230px. If the list exceeds this height, it becomes scrollable. This max-height is configurable.
- The height of each item in the option list is flexible. Long labels will wrap into multiple lines.

### Basic Combobox

```tsx
<ComboboxWithState {...args} />
```

### With option description

Optional descriptions can be included for items in the option list when additional context will help users make more confident choices. For example, you might display a list of first and last names alongside their corresponding email addresses. Note that the type-to-filter functionality does not consider these descriptions.

```tsx
<ComboboxWithState {...args} />
```

### With preselected value

By default, no option will be selected. However, a pre-selected value can be provided.

```tsx
<ComboboxWithState {...args} />
```

### Combobox with multiple selections

The combobox component can handle multiple selections, allowing users to select multiple items as input. The number of selections is displayed as removable tags in the input field, enabling users to clear all selections. This feature is enabled with the `multiple` prop.

Similar to single selection, you can retrieve the new values using the `onChange([values, meta])` callback, which always returns an array. The `value` prop is also an array of values, such as `["1", "2"]`.

```tsx
<MultiSelectComboboxWithState {...args} />
```

### With disabled options

Disabled options cannot be selected or deselected by the user but may be preselected through the value prop. When users navigate through the list using the keyboard, disabled options will be skipped.

```tsx
<ComboboxWithState {...args} />
```

```tsx
<MultiSelectComboboxWithState {...args} />
```

### Option with customField

When custom fields are provided, users can use these as keywords to filter the list of options or find an item by an alternative name that does not match the label. This is useful in cases where the list of options might not be familiar to the users, or when there are multiple common names for an option. This increases the chances that users will find the option they are looking for more quickly.

In the example below, users can find their preferred option both in English and in their native language:

```tsx
<ComboboxWithState {...args} />
```

## Best practices / When to use

- Consider using a combobox when there is a large list of options to choose from, or when the number of options is unknown. For a list of 10 or fewer options or when the choices are familiar to the choices, such as a country selection
- Keep the field label concise and descriptive.
- Use hints to provide more detailed explanations about what to enter in the text input field.
- Order your list of options in a logical manner, such as by the most commonly selected option or numerically.
- Use a the combobox with multiple selections for tasks involving the selection of a few items from a large set of choices. If the task requires selecting many items, consider alternative components such as a selectable entity list.
- If the field is optional, consider including an empty option or an option such as "None" or "Not Applicable." This allows users to clear the selection.

## Accessibility

- Do not use placeholder to clarify field input. Use the field label to indicate which information goes into the input. It is good practice to have the label remain visible at all times.
- The option list is navigable by keyboard. Disabled options will be skipped.

### Keyboard interactions

| Key       | Behavior                                                                                                    |
| --------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `↓` / `↑` | Opens the option list (if closed). Moves focus to the next/previous option.                                 |
| `Enter`   | Selects the focused option and closes the option list.                                                      |
| `Tab`     | Selects the focused option (if any), closes the option list, and moves focus to the next focusable element. |
| `Escape`  | Closes the option list without changing the selection.                                                      |

### ARIA attributes

The combobox follows the [WAI-ARIA Combobox pattern](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/combobox/). Key attributes include `role="combobox"`, `aria-haspopup="listbox"`, `aria-expanded`, and `aria-activedescendant` for screen reader support.

{" "}

<SelectionControls />

<ComboboxSelectDropdownComparison />

**Type Definitions:**

```typescript
export type CommonSelectProps = {
  /** select element name (to be used in form input) */
  name: string;
  /** placeholder to display when no value is selected */
  placeholder?: string;
  /** Hidden message used within aria-live region, when filtering returns no options */
  emptyStateMessage?: string;
  /** Displays control in error state */
  hasError?: boolean;
  /** Disable control */
  disabled?: boolean;
  /** Array of option objects with custom properties along with following properties
   * @param value - Option value
   * @param label - Option label
   * @param description - Optional description
   * @param disabled - Set for disabled option
   * @param alias - Optional alias used for filtering
   */
  options?: SelectOption[];
  /** Provide a custom filter function to filter the options list based on input query */
  filterMethod?: (options: SelectOption, value: string) => boolean;
  /** Multiselect: Provide custom label for trigger Tag in multiselect */
  formatSelectedOptionsLabel?: (count?: number) => string;
  onBlur?: () => void;
  onFocus?: () => void;
  /** Set max height of options list */
  maxHeight?: number;
  /** Custom width for the options list */
  optionsListWidth?: number;
  privateProps?: {
    // remove it once we move Select into Portal
    dropdownPlacement?: "up" | "down";
  };
  /** Multi select combobox */
  multiple?: boolean;
  autoComplete?: string;
  onChange?: (
    selectedValues: SelectOption["value"][] &
      React.ChangeEvent<HTMLSelectElement>,
    actionData?: ActionData,
    e?: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLSelectElement>
  ) => void;
  value: string | string[] | null;
  readOnly?: boolean;
  "aria-describedby"?: string;
} & Pick<PortalProps, "portalContainer">;

type ComboboxMultipleProps = Omit<CommonSelectProps, "multiple"> &
  FormFieldProps & {
    multiple: true;
    slotProps: Omit<ComboboxSlotProps, "tag"> & {
      tag: NonNullable<ComboboxSlotProps["tag"]>;
    };
  };

type ComboboxSingleProps = Omit<CommonSelectProps, "multiple"> &
  FormFieldProps & {
    multiple?: false;
    slotProps?: Omit<ComboboxSlotProps, "tag"> & { tag?: never };
  };

export type ComboboxProps = ComboboxMultipleProps | ComboboxSingleProps;

const defaultFilterMethod = (option: SelectOption, value: string): boolean =>
  option.label.toLowerCase().indexOf(value.toLowerCase()) > -1;

export function Combobox({
  options = [],
  name,
  value,
  placeholder,
  emptyStateMessage,
  hasError,
  filterMethod = defaultFilterMethod,
  formatSelectedOptionsLabel,
  onChange = () => {},
  onBlur = () => null,
  onFocus = () => null,
  maxHeight = 230,
  autoComplete = "on",
  multiple = false,
  privateProps,
  portalContainer,
  optionsListWidth,
  readOnly,
  errorMessages,
  hint,
  slotProps,
  "aria-describedby": ariaDescribedBy,
  "data-e2e-test-id": dataE2eTestId,
  ...rest
}: ComboboxProps): React.ReactElement {
  const { disabled, label } = rest;
  const { tag } = slotProps ?? {};
  const { id, ariaDescribedByProp } = useFormFieldMessageId({
    ariaDescribedBy,
    errorMessages,
    hint,
  });

  return (
    <FormField
      data-ds-id="Combobox"
      disabled={disabled}
      errorMessages={errorMessages}
      hint={hint}
      messageId={id}
      data-e2e-test-id={dataE2eTestId}
      {...rest}
    >
      {multiple ? (
        <MultiSelect
          name={name}
          options={options}
          value={value as string[]}
          placeholder={placeholder}
          emptyStateMessage={emptyStateMessage}
          hasError={hasError}
          disabled={disabled}
          filterMethod={filterMethod}
          onChange={onChange as MultiOnChange}
          onFocus={onFocus}
          onBlur={onBlur}
          maxHeight={maxHeight}
          autoComplete={autoComplete}
          label={label}
          formatSelectedOptionsLabel={formatSelectedOptionsLabel}
          portalContainer={portalContainer}
          optionsListWidth={optionsListWidth}
          slotProps={{ tag }}
          {...ariaDescribedByProp}
          {...rest}
        />
      ) : (
        <SingleSelect
          name={name}
          options={options}
          value={value as string}
          placeholder={placeholder}
          emptyStateMessage={emptyStateMessage}
          hasError={hasError}
          disabled={disabled}
          filterMethod={filterMethod}
          onChange={onChange as SingleOnChange}
          onFocus={onFocus}
          onBlur={onBlur}
          maxHeight={maxHeight}
          autoComplete={autoComplete}
          label={label}
          privateProps={privateProps}
          portalContainer={portalContainer}
          optionsListWidth={optionsListWidth}
          readOnly={readOnly}
          {...ariaDescribedByProp}
          {...rest}
        />
      )}
    </FormField>
  );
}
```


### DateInput

# Date input

Date input is a special case of <a href="/?path=/docs/form-maskedinput--docs" target="_blank">MaskedInput</a> with predefined mask for commonly used date formats.

## Date input with predefined formats

```tsx
<Stack>
      <DateInput {...args} name="us" label="US format" format="US" />
      <DateInput {...args} name="de" label="DE format" format="DE" />
      <DateInput {...args} name="row" label="RoW format" format="RoW" />
    </Stack>
```

## Date input with custom format and placeholder

You can provide a custom mask with chars `d`, `m`, `Y` representing day, month and year respectively. Use the backtick ``` char after seperator to avoid digit shift back while editing individual parts of the date.

**Type Definitions:**

```typescript
export type DateInputProps = {
  format: "US" | "DE" | "RoW" | string;
  isPlaceholderShown?: boolean;
  dayPlaceholderChar?: string;
  monthPlaceholderChar?: string;
  yearPlaceholderChar?: string;
  "data-e2e-test-id"?: string;
} & Omit<MaskedInputProps, "maskOptions">;
```


### DatePicker

# DatePicker

DatePicker is a masked date input with a calendar icon button. It reuses the same date mask logic as [DateInput](/?path=/docs/form-dateinput--docs) (via shared [MaskedInput](/?path=/docs/form-maskedinput--docs) / IMask).

- **Formats:** US (MM/DD/YYYY), DE (DD.MM.YYYY), RoW (DD/MM/YYYY). Default is US.
- **Direct entry:** Users can type the date directly into the input; no calendar required.
- **Calendar icon:** The trailing button shows a calendar icon and opens an inline calendar popover for picking a date.

> **Value callbacks (IMask):** DatePicker works as an **uncontrolled** component for the masked value. Optional **`onAccept(value, unmaskedValue, typedValue)`** fires whenever the mask value changes—on each keystroke and when a date is picked from the calendar. Optional **`onComplete(value, unmaskedValue, typedValue)`** fires only when the mask is completely filled. Both receive: `value` (masked, e.g. `"12/25/2025"`), `unmaskedValue` (digits only), and `typedValue`.

## Limiting the allowed year range

You can limit the calendar year dropdown via the IMask `maskBlocks` configuration:

- **`maskBlocks.Y.from` / `maskBlocks.Y.to`** — Clamps the year range for **both** the input mask and the calendar year dropdown.

Example using `maskBlocks` (affects input and dropdown):

```tsx
<DatePicker
  format="US"
  maskBlocks={{
    Y: {
      from: 2000,
      to: 2040,
    },
  }}
  slotProps={{
    calendarButton: {
      ariaLabelOpen: "Open calendar",
      ariaLabelClose: "Close calendar",
    },
    calendar: {
      ariaLabelMonth: "Month",
      ariaLabelYear: "Year",
      ariaLabelCalendarGrid: "Calendar grid",
      formatAriaLabel: (monthName, year) =>
        `${monthName} ${year}. Use arrow keys to move, Enter or Space to select.`,
    },
  }}
/>
```

This configuration:

- Prevents typing years outside 2000–2040 in the input.
- Restricts the calendar’s year dropdown to the same range, so mask and calendar stay in sync.

## Default (US format)

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <DatePicker {...props} />
    </div>
```

## With error

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <DatePicker {...props} />
    </div>
```

## All formats

```tsx
<Stack>
      <div style={{ width: 300 }}>
        <DatePicker {...args} name="us" label="US format" format="US" />
      </div>
      <div style={{ width: 300 }}>
        <DatePicker {...args} name="de" label="DE format" format="DE" />
      </div>
      <div style={{ width: 300 }}>
        <DatePicker {...args} name="row" label="RoW format" format="RoW" />
      </div>
    </Stack>
```

## Event logging

Use the **Event logging** story to try `onAccept` and `onComplete` in the canvas: type in the input to see **Last onAccept** update on each change; complete a full date (e.g. 12/25/2025) to see **Last onComplete** fire once.

```tsx
<EventLoggingDemo {...props} />
```

## Related

- [DateInput](/?path=/docs/form-dateinput--docs) – masked date input without icon or popover
- [MaskedInput](/?path=/docs/form-maskedinput--docs) – base masking with IMask


### FormFieldGroup

# FormFieldGroup

Disable state is only visual representation. Please, disable inputs inside separately.

- **Do** use `<FormFieldGroup />` only for form elements.

## Props

## Basic FormFieldGroup usage

This is very basic usage of FormFieldGroup.

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 800 }}>
      <FormFieldGroup {...props}>
        <Checkbox label="option1" name="grouped" />
        <Checkbox label="option2" name="grouped" />
        <Checkbox label="option3" name="grouped" />
      </FormFieldGroup>
    </div>
```

## Group everything

Any form elements could be grouped.
But it's usually a good idea to group inputs without vertical label.

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 800 }}>
      <Stack space="xxl">
        <FormFieldGroup {...props}>
          <Input
            name="grouped"
            label="Date"
            hasError
            value=""
            onChange={() => null}
          />
          <Input name="grouped" label="Time" value="" onChange={() => null} />
          <Select
            label="&nbsp;"
            name="grouped"
            value="AM"
            options={[
              { value: "AM", label: "AM" },
              { value: "PM", label: "PM" },
            ]}
            onChange={() => null}
          />
        </FormFieldGroup>
      </Stack>
    </div>
```

## With error and hint

FormFieldGroup accepts an array of error messages, that will be displayed in stack.

> Note that hint and errors can't be set together. Error messages always have a priority.

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 800 }}>
      <FormFieldGroup {...props}>
        <Checkbox label="option1" name="grouped" />
        <Checkbox label="option2" name="grouped" />
        <Checkbox label="option3" name="grouped" />
      </FormFieldGroup>
    </div>
```

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 800 }}>
      <FormFieldGroup {...props}>
        <Checkbox label="option1" name="grouped" />
        <Checkbox label="option2" name="grouped" />
        <Checkbox label="option3" name="grouped" />
      </FormFieldGroup>
    </div>
```

## Disable state

> Important: Disable state is only visual representation. Please disable inputs inside separately.

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 800 }}>
      <FormFieldGroup {...props}>
        <Checkbox label="option1" name="grouped" />
        <Checkbox label="option2" name="grouped" />
        <Checkbox label="option3" name="grouped" />
      </FormFieldGroup>
    </div>
```

**Type Definitions:**

```typescript
type ChildProps = {
  disabled?: boolean;
  [key: string]: any;
};

export type FormFieldGroupProps = {
  label?: string;
  labelAs?: ElementType<any, "div" | "h1" | "h2" | "h3" | "h4" | "h5" | "h6">;
  isLabelHidden?: boolean;
  labelHint?: string;
  children: React.ReactNode[] | React.ReactElement;
  errorMessages?: string[];
  hint?: string;
  disabled?: boolean;
  /* Display form elements inline if they can fit in container */
  inline?: boolean;
  /* Horizontal spacing between form elements */
  space?: SpaceSizes | MQ<SpaceSizes>;
  "data-e2e-test-id"?: string;
};

type StyledFieldsetProps = Partial<FormFieldGroupProps> & {
  isObserverDone: boolean;
};
```


### Input

# Input

Since input component is just a visual, it's expected from a consumer to
create a wrapper in order to align with form framework of choice.

## Props

## Basic Text Input

This is very basic usage of Input.

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <Input {...props} />
    </div>
```

## Basic Text Input With placeholder

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <Input {...props} />
    </div>
```

## Basic Disabled Text Input

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <Input {...props} />
    </div>
```

## Number Input

This is very basic usage of number type Input.

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <Input {...props} />
    </div>
```

## Input With Label hint

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <Input {...props} />
    </div>
```

## Input with hint

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <Input {...props} />
    </div>
```

## Input with error

Input accepts an array of error messages, that will be displayed in stack.

> Note that hint and errors can't be set together. Error messages always have a priority.

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <Input {...props} />
    </div>
```

## Grouping

All you need to do is to wrap all your inputs with **[FormFieldGroup](?path=/docs/form-formfieldgroup--docs)** component.

> It's usually a good idea to give all the inputs the same name.

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <FormFieldGroup
        label="Input group"
        labelHint="OPTIONAL"
        errorMessages={["Shared error one", "Shared error two"]}
      >
        <Input
          label="input1"
          name="grouped"
          value=""
          onChange={() => null}
          hasError
        />
        <Input
          hasError
          label="input2"
          name="grouped"
          value=""
          onChange={() => null}
        />
        <Input label="input3" name="grouped" value="" onChange={() => null} />
      </FormFieldGroup>
    </div>
```

**Type Definitions:**

```typescript
export type InputProps = {
  icon?: IconName;
  size?: InputTextSize; // TODO: This needs to be renamed as it overrides size from HTML attributes
  /**
   * Set to true to toggle error state
   */
  hasError?: boolean;
  privateProps?: { isTransparent?: boolean; hideOutline?: boolean };
} & FormFieldProps &

type StyledInputProps = {
  inputSize: InputProps["size"];
} & Omit<InputProps, "size">;
```


### MaskedInput

# MaskedInput

MaskedInput enhances <a href="/?path=/docs/form-input--docs" target="_blank">Input</a> component with masking abilities. Input masking restricts what the user can enter and also auto formats it. This feature is implemented using <a href="https://imask.js.org/guide.html" target="_blank">IMask</a> library. IMask supports several kinds of masks, number, pattern, date, range etc. Please check its documentation for usage and examples.

> Note: MaskedInput works as an **uncontrolled** component. Use `onAccept` instead of `onChange` to retrieve input value as it changes.

## Examples

Here are some examples of using IMask options to construct data inputs for a specific kind of value.

### Date mask

### Time mask

```tsx
<Columns gap="s">
      <Column size={[12, 5, 5]}>
        <MaskedInput {...args} />
      </Column>
      <Column size={[12, 4, 4]}>
        <Select
          name="am-pm"
          value="am"
          label="&nbsp;"
          options={[
            { value: "am", label: "AM" },
            { value: "pm", label: "PM" },
          ]}
          onChange={() => {}}
        />
      </Column>
    </Columns>
```

### Credit card number mask

### Currency mask

## Related components

{/* prettier-ignore */}
- <a href="/?path=/docs/form-input--docs" target="_blank">Input</a>

**Type Definitions:**

```typescript
export type MaskedInputProps = {
  /* IMask options object */
  maskOptions: FactoryArg;
  /* Callback to receive value, unmasked value and typed value as it changes */
  onAccept?: (value: string, unmaskedValue: string, typedValue: string) => void;
  /* Callback fired when the mask is completely filled */
  onComplete?: (
    value: string,
    unmaskedValue: string,
    typedValue: string
  ) => void;
} & Omit<InputProps, "type" | "onChange" | "size">;
```


### PasswordInput

# PasswordInput

Since input component is just a visual, it's expected from a consumer to
create a wrapper in order to align with form framework of choice.

## Props

## Password Input

This is very basic usage of password Input.

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <PasswordInput
        {...props}
        label="Password input label"
        slotProps={{
          toggleVisibility: {
            ariaLabelShow: "Show Password Visibility",
            ariaLabelHide: "Hide Password Visibility",
          },
        }}
      />
    </div>
```

**Type Definitions:**

```typescript
export type PasswordInputProps = {
  /**
   * Props for sub-components.
   *
   * - `toggleVisibility.ariaLabelShow` — Label for screen readers when the icon can be toggled to show the password
   *
   * - `toggleVisibility.ariaLabelHide` — Label for screen readers when the icon can be toggled to hide the password
   */
  slotProps: {
    toggleVisibility: {
      /** Label for screen readers when icon can be toggled to show password */
      ariaLabelShow: string;
      /** Label for screen readers when icon can be toggled to hide password */
      ariaLabelHide: string;
    };
  };
} & InputProps;

type InputProps = {
  /**
   * Set to true to toggle error state
   */
  hasError?: boolean;
  /**
   * Function for on icon click behavior
   */
  onIconClick?: (e: React.MouseEvent) => void;
} & FormFieldProps &
```


### Radio

# Radio

A radio is an input control that allows user to choose one option from a limited set of choices.

## Anatomy

1. **Radio**
   The selection control. It also displays whether an option has been selected or not.
2. **Radio label**
   Use this text label to describe what the radio is for. Keep the labels short and descriptive.
3. **Label hint (optional)**
   An additional text element can supplement the radio label when a more detailed description is needed.
4. **Group label**
   When presented in a group, a group label can be used to explain the group’s context.
5. **Group hint**
   Additional information accompanying a radio group.
6. **Group error message**
   An error message for a radio group. The message should clearly describe what is wrong.

## Props

## General properties & Examples

- Long radio labels will be wrapped into multiple lines.
- The interactive area of a radio includes its label and label hint.
- Label hints can contain links and will wrap into multiple lines if long.

### Basic radio

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <Radio {...props} />
    </div>
```

### Size S

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <Radio {...props} />
    </div>
```

### With label hint

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <Radio {...props} />
    </div>
```

### Radio group

Use <a href="/?path=/docs/form-formfieldgroup--docs" target="_blank">FormFieldGroup</a> to wrap a list of radios. FormFieldGroup accepts an array of error messages that will be displayed in stack.

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <FormFieldGroup
        label="Group label"
        hint="Optional group hint is visible even if error is present."
        disabled={args.disabled}
        errorMessages={["Group error message 1.", "Group error message 2."]}
      >
        <Radio label="Option 1" labelHint="Optional label hint" name="test" />
        <Radio label="Option 2" labelHint="Optional label hint" name="test" />
        <Radio label="Option 3" name="test" />
      </FormFieldGroup>
    </div>
```

### Inline radio group

In cases where the radio labels are short and concise and a more compact layout is required, the radio options can be presented next to each other horizontally. To achieve this layout, pass the `inline` prop to <a href="/?path=/docs/form-formfieldgroup--docs" target="_blank">FormFieldGroup</a>. If the width of the group exceeds the width of the parent, the layout will automatically switch to a stacked arrangement.

```tsx
<div
      style={{
        width: 400,
        padding: "20px",
        resize: "horizontal",
        overflowX: "scroll",
      }}
    >
      <FormFieldGroup label="Group label" inline>
        <Radio label="Option 1" name="grouped" />
        <Radio label="Option 2" name="grouped" />
        <Radio label="Option 3" name="grouped" />
      </FormFieldGroup>
    </div>
```

<SelectionControls />

**Type Definitions:**

```typescript
export type RadioProps = {
  label?: string | React.ReactElement;
  /** Display hint below the label specific for the radio */
  labelHint?: string | React.ReactElement;
  size?: RadioSizes; // TODO: This needs to be renamed as it overrides size from HTML attributes
} & Omit<FormFieldProps, "labelHint" | "hideLabel" | "label" | "labelHint"> &

type RadioRawProps = RadioProps & { labelSize?: RadioSizes };
```


### RadioButton

# RadioButton

### Basic RadioButton usage

This is very basic usage of RadioButton.

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <RadioButton label={label} icon={icon} {...(rest as RadioButtonProps)} />
    </div>
```

### With error and hint

RadioButton accepts an array of error messages, that will be displayed in stack.

> Note that hint and errors can't be set together. Error messages always have a priority.

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <RadioButton label={label} icon={icon} {...(rest as RadioButtonProps)} />
    </div>
```

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <RadioButton label={label} icon={icon} {...(rest as RadioButtonProps)} />
    </div>
```

### Grouping

All you need to do is to wrap all your radiobuttons with `<FormFieldGroup />` component.

> It's usually a good idea to give all the radiobuttons the same name.

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <FormFieldGroup
        label="Radio group"
        labelHint="OPTIONAL"
        errorMessages={["Shared error one", "Shared error two"]}
      >
        <RadioButton label="option1" name="one" icon="menu" />
        <RadioButton label="option2" name="one" icon="link" />
        <RadioButton label="option3" name="one" icon="mail" />
      </FormFieldGroup>
    </div>
```

**Type Definitions:**

```typescript
export type RadioButtonProps = {
  name: string;
  value?: string;
  checked?: boolean | undefined;
  icon?: IconName;
  label?: string;
  disabled?: boolean;
  onChange?: (e: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => void;
  onClick?: (e: React.FormEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => void;
  onBlur?: (e: React.FormEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => void;
  onFocus?: (e: React.FormEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => void;
} & Omit<FormFieldProps, "labelHint">;
```


### RangeInput

# Range input

A range input allows the user to specify a numeric value within a given range using a slider.

## Props

## States

### Basic range input

### Range input with label and hint

### Range input with error message

### Disabled range input

## Best practice / When to use

- Use the range input when you need to display a selection of a single value within a given range.
- In its simplest form, the range input can be imprecise. Consider using it in combination with a number input and displaying the minimum and maximum values.
- Avoid using for extremely large or small ranges, such as 1-1000 or 1-3, respectively.

## Accessibility

- Always use with a label, even if that label is hidden.

**Type Definitions:**

```typescript
export type RangeInputProps = {
  /** The step size for each change in the range input's value. */
  hasError?: boolean;
  onSlideStart?: (e: React.FormEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => void;
} & FormFieldProps &
```


### SearchInput

# SearchInput

SearchInput lets users input a search term.

## Props

## Stories

### Basic

Basic SearchInput comes with an input and a clear button when input is given.

```tsx
<SearchInputWithState {...props} />
```

**Type Definitions:**

```typescript
export type SearchInputProps = Omit<InputProps, "type" | "icon"> & {
  /** Clears the input. */
  onClear?: (e: MouseEvent<HTMLButtonElement>) => void;
  slotProps?: {
    clearButton?: {
      ariaLabel?: string;
    };
  };
};
```


### SegmentedControl

# Segmented Control

Segmented control is used to provide users with closely related choices that affect an object, state, or view. The number of options should be limited, and the labels of each option should be concise and have a consistent length.

## Anatomy

1. **Control container**The container acts as a bar that holds all segments together. There are two variants of this: Basic and Justified. See details and examples below.
2. **Selected / active segment**The active segment is visually distinguishable from the rest, clearly indicating that an option has been selected.
3. **Segment label**The concise labels present related choices.
4. **Segment sub-label (optional)**Optional sub-labels can be used to provide additional details or quantifiers that assist users in making informed decisions.

## Props

## Variants

### Basic

The width of each segment in the basic variant is determined by the content inside it. This variant is recommended to be used as a view selector, or to present 2 or 3 options that the user can switch between.

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 600 }}>
      <SegmentedControlWithState {...args} />
    </div>
```

### Justified

The container for the justified segmented control will span across the content view, and each segment will have equal width.
On mobile breakpoints, the segments will be stacked vertically, providing users with larger tap targets.

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 600 }}>
      <SegmentedControlWithState {...args} />
    </div>
```

### Justified with sub-label

Use this variant when providing additional details or quantifiers will help users make informed decisions. For example, presenting different subscription periods with their accompanying prices provides transparency and helps users make their purchasing choices more confidently.

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 600 }}>
      <SegmentedControlWithState {...args} />
    </div>
```

### Disabled Option

The Option will be disabled if you pass the option prop disabled as true. However the tooltip will still work if provided

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 600 }}>
      <SegmentedControlWithState {...args} />
    </div>
```

### Segment label with icon

By default, we recommend displaying only textual labels in segments. However, in certain situations, supplementing them with icons can assist users in identifying choices and making selections more efficiently. When appropriate, use the `icon` prop to add icons into segments.

Furthermore, in rare cases where screen space is limited and icons can effectively represent options (such as a list view versus a grid view), you can consider displaying only icons. This approach takes up less screen space and can also serve as a responsive alternative to using SegmentedControl with textual labels.

Even if you decide to display icons only, the `Label` for each segment is still required. This label is used to create an accessible name for the button. We also recommend displaying <a href="/?path=/docs/components-tooltip--docs" target="_blank">tooltips</a> on hover to expose their readable names.

```tsx
{
    const optionsForIconOnly = args.options.map((option) => ({
      ...option,
      iconOnly: true,
      tooltipContent: option.label,
    }));
    return (
      <Inline space="xxl">
        <SegmentedControlWithState {...args} />
        <SegmentedControlWithState
          {...args}
          options={optionsForIconOnly as SegmentedControlProps["options"]}
        />
      </Inline>
    )
```

## Content guidelines

- Labels should be written in sentence case. As a general practice, labels should be clear and concise.
- In each segment, only one supplemental element should be displayed: either a sub-label or an icon. Adding too many elements creates visual noise and makes it difficult for users to recognize the options. If both sub-labels and icons are provided, only the sub-labels will be displayed.
- Each segment of a SegmentedControl should contain the same content elements. Avoid displaying different combinations of content next to each other, such as displaying an icon-only segment mixed with segments that have sub-labels.

## Best practices / When to use

- Selections are mutually exclusive. Once a selection has been made, it can only be deselected by making a new selection.
- Controls with more than five segments or longer segment labels are not likely to work well on smaller viewports. If there are more than 5 options, use a <a href="/?path=/docs/form-select--docs" target="_blank">select</a> component instead.
- A segmented control does not require an explicit action to confirm the selection. Instead, when a choice is selected, it immediately affects an object, state, or view.
- Do not use a segmented control if the segments are not related to each other.
- Do not use a segmented control as a filter to narrow down a list.
- Do not use a segmented control as a form of navigation. Instead, use <a href="/?path=/docs/components-tabs--docs" target="_blank">tabs</a>.

<SelectionControls />

**Type Definitions:**

```typescript
type StyledDividerProps = {
  isChecked: boolean;
  isDisabled: boolean;
  isPrevOptionChecked: boolean;
  isPrevOptionDisabled: boolean;
} & Pick<SegmentedControlProps, "justified">;

type StyledContainerProps = Pick<
  SegmentedControlProps,
  "justified" | "options"
>;

const StyledContainer = styled.div<StyledContainerProps>(
  ({ justified, options }) => {
    const baseStyle = {
      display: "flex",
    };

    if (!justified) {
      return baseStyle;
    }

    return {
      ...baseStyle,
      flexDirection: "column",

      "& > *": {
        flex: `1 1 ${100 / options.length}%`,
      },

      [`& > ${StyledDivider}`]: {
        flex: "0 0 auto",
      },

      // Layout on tablet and desktop
      [`@media (min-width: ${breakpoints.m.value}px)`]: {
        flexDirection: "row",
      },
    };
  }
```


### Select

# Select

A select input enables users to choose a single option from a list of choices. It is particularly useful when there are more than 5 or 6 options. For larger lists, where filtering could help users find their choice more quickly, consider using a <a href="/?path=/docs/form-combobox--docs" target="_blank">combobox</a> instead.

## Anatomy

1. **Field label**
   Describes the purpose of the select input.
2. **Select input**
   When clicked, the list of choices appears. Once an option is selected, it is shown in input.
3. **Label hint (optional)**
   Additional text that supplements the field label when more context is needed.
4. **Option list**
   Contains all selectable items. Optional descriptions can be displayed for each item.
5. **Selected option indicator**
   A checkmark is shown next to the selected option in a select input. In a multi-selection variant, checkboxes in front of the options act as selection indicators.
6. **Hint**
   Can be used to provide additional information to help users make their choice faster.
7. **Error message**
   Clearly describes what is wrong when there is an error.

## Props

## General properties & Examples

- The width of a select input is defined by its parent container.
- The label and placeholder text can be defined.
- By default, the option list container has a max-height of 230px. If the list exceeds this height, it becomes scrollable. This max-height is configurable.
- The height of each item in the option list is flexible. Long labels will wrap into multiple lines.

### Basic Select

```tsx
<SelectWithState {...args} />
```

### With option description

Optional descriptions can be provided for items in the option list when additional context will help users make more confident choices. For example, you might accompany a list of first and last names with email addresses.

```tsx
<SelectWithState {...args} />
```

### With preselected value

By default, no option will be selected. However, a pre-selected value can be provided.

```tsx
<SelectWithState {...args} />
```

### With disabled options

Disabled options cannot be selected or deselected by the user but may be preselected through the value prop. When users navigate through the list using the keyboard, disabled options will be skipped.

```tsx
<SelectWithState {...args} />
```

### Select with custom trigger

Use `SelectWithCustomTrigger` component to render a Select with a trigger of your choice. For example, here's an example with a tertiary button trigger.

## Best practices / When to use

- Consider using a select when there are more than 5 or 6 options to choose from.
- Keep the field label concise and descriptive.
- Use hints to provide more detailed explanations about what to enter in the input field.
- Order your list of options in a logical manner, such as by the most commonly selected option or numerically.
- If the field is optional, consider including an empty option or an option such as "None" or "Not Applicable." This allows users to clear the selection.
- For larger lists, where filtering could help users find their choice more quickly, consider using a <a href="/?path=/docs/form-combobox--docs" target="_blank">combobox</a> instead.

## Accessibility

- Do not use placeholder to clarify field input. Use the field label to indicate which information goes into the input. It is good practice to have the label remain visible at all times.
- The option list is navigable by keyboard. Disabled options will be skipped.

<SelectionControls />

<ComboboxSelectDropdownComparison />

**Type Definitions:**

```typescript
type StyledButtonProps = Pick<
  TriggerButtonProps,
  "hasError" | "isPlaceholderVisible" | "readOnly" | "size"
>;

const StyledButton = styled.button<StyledButtonProps>(
  ({ theme, hasError, isPlaceholderVisible, readOnly, size }) => ({
    [`&[type="button"]`]: {
      // increase specificity
      appearance: "none",
      MozAppearance: "none",
      WebkitAppearance: "none",
    },
    display: "inline-block",
    margin: 0,
    borderRadius: theme.variables.size.borderRadius.xs,
    backgroundColor: theme.values.color.background.primary.default,
    borderWidth: "1px",
    borderStyle: "solid",
    width: "100%",
    padding: `${
      size === "s"
        ? `calc(${theme.variables.size.spacing.xxs} + 1px)`
        : `calc(${theme.variables.size.spacing.xs} - 1px)`
    } ${theme.variables.size.spacing.s}`,
    paddingRight: theme.variables.size.spacing.xs,
    boxSizing: "border-box",
    borderColor: theme.values.color.border.primary.default,

    ...(readOnly && {
      borderColor: theme.values.color.border.primary.disabled,
    }),

    ...(isPlaceholderVisible && {
      fontStyle: "italic",
    }),

    ...(hasError && {
      boxShadow: `0 0 0 1px inset ${theme.values.color.border.error.default}`,
      borderColor: theme.values.color.border.error.default,
    }),
  })

type TriggerButtonProps = {
  children: string;
  isPlaceholderVisible: boolean;
} & Parameters<SelectWithCustomTriggerProps["renderTrigger"]>[0] &

export type SelectProps = {
  placeholder?: string;
  hasError?: boolean;
  size?: "s" | "m";
  "aria-describedby"?: string;
  onFocus?: FocusEventHandler<HTMLButtonElement>;
  onBlur?: FocusEventHandler<HTMLButtonElement>;
} & Omit<SelectWithCustomTriggerProps, "renderTrigger"> &
```


### Textarea

# Textarea

Since input component is just a visual, it's expected from a consumer to
create a wrapper in order to align with form framework of choice.

### Basic Textarea

This is very basic usage of Textarea.

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <Textarea label={label} {...(rest as TextareaProps)} />
    </div>
```

### Basic Textarea Disabled

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <Textarea label={label} {...(rest as TextareaProps)} />
    </div>
```

### Textarea With Label hint

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <Textarea label={label} {...(rest as TextareaProps)} />
    </div>
```

### Textarea With hint

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <Textarea label={label} {...(rest as TextareaProps)} />
    </div>
```

### Textarea with error hint

Input accepts an array of error messages, that will be displayed in stack.

> Note that hint and errors can't be set together. Error messages always have a priority.

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <Textarea label={label} {...(rest as TextareaProps)} />
    </div>
```

### Textarea with resize horizontal

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <Textarea label={label} {...(rest as TextareaProps)} />
    </div>
```

### Textarea with resize vertical

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <Textarea label={label} {...(rest as TextareaProps)} />
    </div>
```

### Textarea with resize both

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <Textarea label={label} {...(rest as TextareaProps)} />
    </div>
```

### Textarea with set rows

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <Textarea label={label} {...(rest as TextareaProps)} />
    </div>
```

**Type Definitions:**

```typescript
type TextareaRawProps = {
  /**
   * Set to true to toggle error state
   */
  hasError?: boolean;
  resize?: "none" | "horizontal" | "vertical" | "both";
} & TextareaHTMLAttributes<HTMLTextAreaElement>;

export type TextareaProps = FormFieldProps & TextareaRawProps;

const StyledTextarea = styled.textarea<Partial<TextareaProps>>(
  ({ theme, resize, hasError }) => ({
    fontFamily: theme.variables.fontFamily.lato,
    fontSize: theme.variables.size.font.m,
    lineHeight: theme.variables.size.lineHeight.xl,
    minHeight: theme.variables.size.dimension.textarea.minHeight.m,
    borderRadius: theme.variables.size.borderRadius.xs,
    border: "1px solid",
    width: "100%",
    padding: theme.variables.size.spacing.xs,
    boxSizing: "border-box",
    color: theme.values.color.text.primary.default,
    borderColor: hasError
      ? theme.values.color.border.error.default
      : theme.values.color.border.primary.default,
    backgroundColor: theme.values.color.background.primary.default,
    ...(hasError && {
      boxShadow: `0 0 0 1px inset ${theme.values.color.border.error.default}`,
    }),
    resize,
  })
```


### Toggle

# Toggle

A toggle is an input control that allows users to switch between two states, typically "on" and "off". Toggles are commonly used for settings and preferences where users need to activate or deactivate a feature.

## Anatomy

1. **Toggle**
   The selection control. It also displays whether an option has been selected or not.
2. **Toggle label**
   Use this text label to describe what the toggle is for. Keep the labels short and descriptive.
3. **Label hint (optional)**
   An additional text element can supplement the toggle label when a more detailed description is needed.
4. **Group label**
   When presented in a group, a group label can be used to explain the group’s context.
5. **Group hint**
   Additional information accompanying a toggle group.
6. **Group error message**
   An error message for a toggle group. The message should clearly describe what is wrong.

## Props

## General properties & Examples

- Toggles are available in two sizes: M (default) and S.
- By default, the toggle labels appear before the toggle switch. However, there is an option to set the label to appear after the toggle, which is the preferred option for desktop user interfaces.
- The interactive area of a toggle includes its label and label hint.
- Long toggle labels will wrap into multiple lines.
- Label hints can contain links and will wrap into multiple lines if long.

### Basic toggle

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <Toggle {...args} />
    </div>
```

### Label after

This is the preferred option for creating desktop UIs, particularly when multiple toggles are displayed. It ensures better visual alignment and makes the options more easily scannable.

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <Toggle {...args} />
    </div>
```

### Size S

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <Toggle {...args} />
    </div>
```

### With label hint

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <Toggle {...args} />
    </div>
```

### Toggle group

Use <a href="/?path=/docs/form-formfieldgroup--docs" target="_blank">FormFieldGroup</a> to wrap a list of toggles. FormFieldGroup accepts an array of error messages that will be displayed in stack

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 300 }}>
      <FormFieldGroup
        label="Group label"
        hint="Optional group hint is visible even if error is present."
        disabled={args.disabled}
        errorMessages={["Group error message 1.", "Group error message 2."]}
      >
        <Toggle
          label="Option 1"
          name="one"
          alignItems={args.alignLabel === "before" ? "spaceBetween" : "left"}
          alignLabel={args.alignLabel}
          labelHint="Optional label hint"
        />
        <Toggle
          label="Option 2"
          name="two"
          alignItems={args.alignLabel === "before" ? "spaceBetween" : "left"}
          alignLabel={args.alignLabel}
          labelHint="Optional label hint"
        />
        <Toggle
          label="Option 3"
          name="three"
          alignItems={args.alignLabel === "before" ? "spaceBetween" : "left"}
          alignLabel={args.alignLabel}
        />
      </FormFieldGroup>
    </div>
```

<SelectionControls />

**Type Definitions:**

```typescript
type ToggleRawProps = {
  size?: ToggleSize;
  isHighlighted?: boolean;
} & Omit<InputHTMLAttributes<HTMLInputElement>, "size" | "type">;

export type ToggleProps = ToggleRawProps & {
  /** Controls label alignment with respect to toggle */
  alignLabel?: "before" | "after";
  /** Display hint below the label specific for the toggle */
  labelHint?: string | React.ReactElement;
  alignItems?: InlineProps["alignItems"];
} & Omit<FormFieldProps, "labelHint" | "hideLabel">;
```


### ToggleButton

# ToggleButton

{/* @missedDoc Is toogle button looks as badge? otherwise why it doesn't look as button? */}

### Basic ToggleButton usage

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 800 }}>
      <Card>
        <Box space="xxl" alignText="center">
          <ToggleButton
            label={label}
            subLabel={subLabel}
            name={name}
            {...rest}
          />
        </Box>
      </Card>
    </div>
```

### ToggleButton with sublabel

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 800 }}>
      <Card>
        <Box space="xxl" alignText="center">
          <ToggleButton
            label={label}
            subLabel={subLabel}
            name={name}
            {...rest}
          />
        </Box>
      </Card>
    </div>
```

### ToggleButton group

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 800 }}>
      <Card>
        <Box space="xxl" alignText="center">
          <Inline>
            <ToggleButton {...args} label="Default" name="toggle1" />
            <ToggleButton {...args} label="Active" name="toggle1" checked />
            <ToggleButton {...args} label="Looooong Label" name="toggle1" />
          </Inline>
        </Box>
      </Card>
    </div>
```

**Type Definitions:**

```typescript
export type ToggleButtonProps = {
  label: string;
  subLabel?: string;
} & Omit<
```


### ToolbarToggle

# ToolbarToggle

ToolbarToggle is a single-option on/off toggle built on top of the <a href="/?path=/docs/form-segmentedcontrol--docs" target="_blank">SegmentedControl</a>. It allows users to switch a setting or view between an active and inactive state.

## Props

## Examples

### Basic

```tsx
<ToolbarToggleWithState {...args} />
```

### With icon

```tsx
<ToolbarToggleWithState {...args} />
```

### Icon only

Even when displaying only an icon, the `label` prop is still required. It is used to provide an accessible name for the toggle.

```tsx
<ToolbarToggleWithState {...args} />
```

### Disabled

```tsx
<ToolbarToggleWithState {...args} />
```

### Sizes

ToolbarToggle is available in three sizes: S, M (default), and L.

```tsx
<Inline space="l">
      <ToolbarToggleWithState
        name="small"
        label="Small"
        checked={false}
        size="s"
        onChange={() => {}}
      />
      <ToolbarToggleWithState
        name="medium"
        label="Medium"
        checked={false}
        size="m"
        onChange={() => {}}
      />
      <ToolbarToggleWithState
        name="large"
        label="Large"
        checked={false}
        size="l"
        onChange={() => {}}
      />
    </Inline>
```


### RadioCard

# RadioCard

A radio card is an extended input control, allowing users to select one option from a group of choices.
The extended card allows us to show more information such as a status and accompanying text.

This component is still a WIP. We hope to add sizing support in the future.

## Anatomy

1. **Label**
   The primary text of the card. This is the main content that describes the option. It appears in bold and changes color when selected.
2. **Radio button**
   The radio input control that allows users to select the card. Only one radio card can be selected within a group. Clicking anywhere on the card will select the radio button.
3. **Custom content area**
   An optional area on the right side of the card where custom content can be rendered using the `renderContent` prop. This could include icons, badges, or other UI elements.
4. **Label hint** (optional)
   Optional secondary text that provides additional context or detailed information to complement the label. Appears below the main label in a smaller font size.
5. **Card container**
   The entire card area that acts as a clickable surface. The background and border change appearance when the card is selected or hovered. It comes in two different sizes: `s` and `m` (Default)

## Props

## Basic radio card

A simple radio card with just a label.

## With label hint

A radio card that includes additional context through the optional `labelHint` prop.

## With long label and hint

Demonstrates how the radio card handles longer text content for both the main label and hint text.

## Open Link settings

Shows multiple radio cards grouped together using FormFieldGroup for link opening preferences, with custom content rendered via the `renderContent` prop to display relevant icons.

## Course booking

Demonstrates radio cards for course booking with custom status indicators using dot icons and styled text components.

**Type Definitions:**

```typescript
export type RadioCardProps = {
  /* Primary text of the card. Placed next to the radio element. */
  label: string;
  readOnly?: boolean;
  renderContent?: (params: { isSelected: boolean }) => React.ReactNode;
} & Omit<RadioProps, "errorMessages" | "hideLabel" | "hint">;
```


## Layout

### Box

# Box

Box component is used to provide a padding and some alignment to certain content.

- **Do not** use Box to achieve spacing between components (see in examples). Use [**Stack**](?path=/docs/layout-stack--docs) or [**Inline**](?path=/docs/layout-inline--docs) instead.
- **Do not** use `<Box vSpace="m" space="m" />` inside card for padding, for consistency purposes please use [**CardBox**](?path=/story/layout-box--card-box) to achieve that.
- **Do not** use custom paddings (e.g. applied via styles)

## Simple Box Example

```tsx
<div style={{ display: "inline-block", border: `1px solid black` }}>
      <Box {...args}>
        <Inline alignItems="left" vAlignItems="center">
          <Text>This is the Inner content of a box</Text>
          <Button leftIcon="bubble">Button</Button>
        </Inline>
      </Box>
    </div>
  ),
};
```

## Card Box Example

```tsx
<div style={{ width: 800, height: 250 }}>
      <Card {...rest}>{children}</Card>
    </div>
```

## Responsive Box Example

To make your box responsive, all you have to do is pass an array of 3 values `[value for small screen, value for medium screen, value for large screen]`
`space`, `vSpace` and `alignText` props are responsive.

In this example, the Box has :

- a padding of size **xs**, a vertical padding of size **m** and alignText **left** on **small screens**
- a padding of size **m**, a vertical padding of size **l** and alignText **right** on **medium screens**
- a padding of size **xl**, a vertical padding of size **xxl** and alignText **center** on **large screens**

Therefore :

- `space` has a value of `["xs", "m", "xl"]`
- `vSpace` has a value of `["m", "l", "xxl"]`
- `alignText` has a value of `["left", "right", "center"]`

```tsx
<div style={{ display: "inline-block", border: `1px solid black` }}>
      <Box {...args}>
        <Inline alignItems="left" vAlignItems="center">
          <Text>This is the Inner content of a box</Text>
          <Button leftIcon="bubble">Button</Button>
        </Inline>
      </Box>
    </div>
  ),
};
```

## Nested Boxes Example

Boxes can be nested to achieve different padding and alignment value.

- Each Box has a blue border, the inner content has a red border. Those border were added so you can where the padding starts and where it finishes.

```tsx
<>{args.children}</>
```
